sections.paragraph:
Βηθεσδά, ἡ indecl. (בֵּית חִסְדָּא house of mercy?), name of a pool in Jerusalem J 5:2 v.l. The editions of Tdf., W-H., S., N25 have Βηθζαθά (q.v.) in the text; another v.l. is Βηθσαϊδά. Cp. W-S. §5, 27g; Hdb. ad loc.—See HVincent: Vincent-Abel, Jérusalem II, 1926, Sect. XXVIII Sainte Anne et ses sanctuaires; JJeremias, ZNW 31, ’32, 308–12, Die Wiederentdeckung von Bethesda ’49 (the double pool of St. Anna), Eng. tr., The Rediscovery of Bethesda ’66; CPronobis, Bethesda z. Zt. Jesu: TQ 114, ’33, 181–207; BHHW I, 232f. Perh. בֵּית אֵשְׁדָּה ‘place of outpouring’, cp. the dual form in 3Q 15, 11, 12, JMilik, Discoveries in the Judaean Desert of Jordan III, ’62. 271f, 297; EVardaman, BT 14, ’63, 27–29; DWieand, NTS 12, ’66, 392–404.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Abel Abel = FA., Grammaire du Grec biblique—List 6

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Eng. tr. Eng. tr. = English translation

TQ TQ = Theologische Quartalschrift—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

BT BT =Bible Translator—List 6

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 174
